businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY), net income $3.79B (-46.5% YoY). Closed Tuesday (3/17) at $399.27 (+0.94%). NVIDIA's DRIVE platform announced major partnerships with Uber, BYD, and Hyundai at GTC, directly challenging Tesla's FSD dominance. Electrek reported an imminent large-scale ATM offering to fund Terafab — the first major capital raise since 2020.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 13.6x S&P 500 average. Extreme premium reflecting AI, robotics, energy expectations

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus $2.56. High growth expectations priced in

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $94.8B decline. Delivery drop (-8.6%) and price cuts

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Margin compression from price war with BYD

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Below large-cap tech peers. Margin recovery needed

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral-bearish. Slight bounce but weak momentum

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Launch D-3: First Step in AI Chip Vertical Integration
growthDrivers.description: Logic+memory+packaging in one 2nm fab. 3/21 launch. But Electrek reports imminent ATM offering for $25-40B funding.

growthDrivers.title: LG Energy $4.3B LFP Battery Plant Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: US government confirmed Tesla-LG deal for Megapack 3 US-made cells. Production starting 2027. 5 MWh per unit (+28%).

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April Production Start
growthDrivers.description: Steering-wheel-free autonomous robotaxi. Targeting 2M units/year. Regulatory approval is key hurdle.

growthDrivers.title: China Jan-Feb Sales +35% YoY
growthDrivers.description: 127,728 China-made EVs sold in Jan-Feb, +35% YoY. European Feb registrations also +10% YoY.

growthDrivers.title: Macrohard: Tesla-xAI Enterprise AI Joint Venture
growthDrivers.description: Enterprise-grade generative AI platform. AI hardware+software vertical integration with Terafab.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share. FSD 8.2B miles of data, Supercharger NACS standard, vertical integration. But NVIDIA DRIVE's partnerships with Uber/BYD/Hyundai are cracking the autonomy monopoly.

moats.description: 8.2B FSD miles. But NVIDIA DRIVE catching up with Uber 28-city, BYD, Hyundai partnerships.
Own battery (4680), custom chips (Terafab), software (FSD), charging (Supercharger).
NACS is North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Supercharger.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales #1 (2025: 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M). 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging

competitors.name: NVIDIA DRIVE
competitors.detail: GTC 2026 partnerships with Uber (28 cities), BYD, Hyundai. Most direct FSD competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Commercial robotaxi service expanding. Tech maturity advantage but limited scalability

recentNews.headline: Electrek: Imminent ATM offering for Terafab funding — first major raise since 2020
recentNews.significance: Potential $25-40B capital raise. Short-term dilution pressure but long-term AI chip self-sufficiency

recentNews.headline: NVIDIA DRIVE partners with Uber (28 cities), BYD, and Hyundai
recentNews.significance: Most direct threat to Tesla's FSD monopoly. NVIDIA positioning as 'Android of autonomy'

recentNews.headline: US government confirms Tesla-LG $4.3B LFP battery plant deal
recentNews.significance: Megapack 3 US-made cells from 2027. Energy business supply chain secured

recentNews.headline: S&P 500 +0.25%, market rebounds amid Iran war. Oil (Brent $100+) rising
Terafab scope: logic+memory+packaging under one roof
recentNews.significance: Ambitious 2nm fab with zero semiconductor experience. High risk, high reward

analystOpinions.notableComment: Consensus $406.84 (+1.9%), majority Hold. Imminent ATM offering and NVIDIA DRIVE competition add downside risk.

risks.title: Imminent ATM Offering — Major Dilution Risk
risks.description: First large-scale capital raise since 2020. $25-40B Terafab costs require significant equity issuance.

risks.title: NVIDIA DRIVE Partnerships — Autonomy Monopoly Cracking
risks.description: Uber (28 cities), BYD, Hyundai joining NVIDIA's open platform. Challenges Tesla's closed FSD ecosystem.

risks.title: BYD Competition Accelerating
risks.description: BYD 2025 EV: 2.26M (+28%) vs Tesla 1.64M (-8%). Price war intensifying.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Investigation Expanding
risks.description: 2.88M vehicles under investigation. Austin robotaxi violations. Regulatory hurdle for Cybercab.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation: P/E 368x
risks.description: Further EPS dilution possible with capital raise.

risks.title: Iran War + Oil $100+ — Macro Uncertainty
risks.description: Strait of Hormuz tensions. Consumer spending weakness could hurt EV demand.

buyReasons.title: Terafab D-3 + LG Battery Deal: Energy+AI Expansion
buyReasons.rationale: If Terafab succeeds, first step to NVIDIA independence. LG deal secures energy supply chain.

buyReasons.title: Cybercab + Macrohard: Revenue Diversification
buyReasons.rationale: Robotaxi commercialization and enterprise AI market entry.

buyReasons.title: China Sales +35%, Delivery Rebound Signal
buyReasons.rationale: China recovery signals FY2026 delivery rebound. Energy segment at record margins.

buyReasons.title: Cash $44B, D/E 0.08 — Strong Balance Sheet
buyReasons.rationale: Even with ATM offering, existing cash provides significant buffer.

overallOpinion: Tesla closed at $399.27 (+0.94%) but two key risks surfaced: Electrek reported an imminent ATM offering for Terafab funding — the first major capital raise since 2020. And NVIDIA's DRIVE platform announced partnerships with Uber (28 cities), BYD, and Hyundai, directly challenging Tesla's FSD monopoly.

P/E 368x, below both 50-day ($420) and 200-day ($426) moving averages. RSI 42. Target $407 (+1.9%). Saturday's Terafab launch is the inflection point, but the ATM offering overhang will likely cap upside in the near term. Patience required.
Catalysts are abundant — Terafab, LG battery deal, Cybercab — but the dilution risk and NVIDIA DRIVE competition make this a 'wait and see' moment.

investmentVerdict.summary: Rich catalysts but imminent ATM offering and NVIDIA DRIVE competition create significant headwinds. P/E 368x with potential further dilution. Wait for Terafab launch results and ATM terms.

sources.name: Electrek (Capital Raise)
sources.description: Imminent ATM offering for Terafab funding
